title: Web Developer
description:
**Web Developer Job DescriptionAbout The Company**
 Xurrent is a forward\\-thinking digital solutions provider dedicated to delivering innovative web experiences that drive business growth. Our company specializes in creating scalable, user\\-centric websites and digital platforms tailored to meet the unique needs of our diverse clientele. With a focus on cutting\\-edge technology and design excellence, Xurrent prides itself on fostering a collaborative environment where creativity and technical expertise converge to produce impactful digital solutions. Our team is composed of passionate professionals committed to maintaining the highest standards of quality, efficiency, and client satisfaction. As we continue to expand our global footprint, we are seeking talented individuals who are eager to contribute to our mission of transforming digital landscapes and empowering brands through exceptional web development services.
   

  

**About The Role**
 We are looking for a skilled Webflow Developer to join our dynamic team at Xurrent. In this role, you will serve as the architectural guardian of our primary digital storefront—our website. You will work closely with our global Go\\-To\\-Market (GTM) teams to craft high\\-converting, visually compelling web experiences that align with our strategic marketing objectives. Your expertise will ensure that the website not only looks stunning but also performs optimally across devices and browsers. You will be responsible for building responsive landing pages, microsites, and executing core website updates directly from Figma designs. Additionally, you will oversee site maintenance, implement technical SEO best practices, and support marketing campaigns through seamless integration of tracking and analytics tools. This position offers an exciting opportunity to blend creativity with technical excellence, playing a pivotal role in enhancing our digital presence and supporting our growth initiatives worldwide.
   

  

**Qualifications**
 The ideal candidate will possess over three years of dedicated web development experience, with at least two years specializing in Webflow. A Webflow Certification is highly advantageous. You should have a deep understanding of HTML, CSS, and vanilla JavaScript to customize and optimize web pages effectively. Experience with standard Webflow development frameworks, such as Finsweet's Client\\-First, is highly preferred. Strong practical knowledge of technical SEO fundamentals and the ability to implement these within Webflow is essential. You should demonstrate excellent design literacy, with the capability to translate Figma files into pixel\\-perfect Webflow pages, paying meticulous attention to detail, spacing, and typography. Familiarity with integrating third\\-party tools via APIs, webhooks, or embed codes—such as HubSpot forms, tracking pixels, and automation platforms like Zapier or Make—is necessary. Candidates must be based in or willing to commute to our Bangalore office and possess excellent communication skills to collaborate effectively across different time zones. A compelling portfolio showcasing complex, fast\\-loading, and responsive Webflow sites is required to demonstrate your technical prowess and design sensibility.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Webflow Development: Build responsive, pixel\\-perfect, and visually appealing landing pages, microsites, and website updates directly from Figma designs, ensuring consistency with brand standards and user experience best practices.
* Site Maintenance \\& Health: Monitor and maintain the website’s structure, ensuring compatibility across browsers and devices, fast load times, and a bug\\-free user experience. Regularly clean up unused classes, interactions, and legacy code to optimize site performance.
* Technical SEO Execution: Implement and maintain technical SEO best practices within Webflow, including semantic HTML, structured data, canonical tags, and effective redirect management to enhance search engine visibility.
* GTM Partnership: Collaborate with the marketing team to deploy campaign\\-specific pages swiftly, integrate tracking scripts such as GA4, HubSpot, and LinkedIn Insight Tag, and support lead generation initiatives through technical execution.
* CMS Management: Structure and manage Webflow CMS collections for blogs, events, resources, and integrations to ensure scalability and ease of content management for non\\-technical team members.


**Benefits**
 Xurrent offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to support our employees' well\\-being and professional growth. We provide competitive salary packages, flexible work arrangements, and opportunities for continuous learning and development. Our employees enjoy health insurance coverage, paid time off, and a vibrant work environment that encourages innovation and collaboration. Additionally, we promote work\\-life balance and provide resources to help our team members thrive both professionally and personally. Being part of Xurrent means contributing to impactful projects and working alongside talented professionals in a supportive and growth\\-oriented culture.
   

  

 Equal Opportunity
   

  

 Xurrent is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, or any other protected characteristic. We believe that a diverse workforce fosters innovation and drives our success, and we are dedicated to providing equal employment opportunities to all qualified candidates.

title: Senior Software Engineer, Google Cloud Networking
description:
**Minimum qualifications:**
* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience.
* 5 years of experience with software development in one or more programming languages.
* 3 years of experience testing, maintaining, or launching software products, and 1 year of experience with software design and architecture.


**Preferred qualifications:**
* Master's degree or PhD in Computer Science or a related technical field.
* 5 years of experience with data structures/algorithms.
* 1 year of experience in a technical leadership role.
* Experience developing accessible technologies.


**About The Job**
 Google's software engineers develop the next\\-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect, explore, and interact with information and one another. Our products need to handle information at massive scale, and extend well beyond web search. We're looking for engineers who bring fresh ideas from all areas, including information retrieval, distributed computing, large\\-scale system design, networking and data storage, security, artificial intelligence, natural language processing, UI design and mobile; the list goes on and is growing every day. As a software engineer, you will work on a specific project critical to Google’s needs with opportunities to switch teams and projects as you and our fast\\-paced business grow and evolve. We need our engineers to be versatile, display leadership qualities and be enthusiastic to take on new problems across the full\\-stack as we continue to push technology forward.
   

  

 In this role, you will manage project priorities, deadlines, and deliverables. You will design, develop, test, deploy, maintain, and enhance software solutions.
   

  

 The AI and Infrastructure team is redefining what’s possible. We empower Google customers with breakthrough capabilities and insights by delivering AI and Infrastructure at unparalleled scale, efficiency, reliability and velocity. Our customers include Googlers, Google Cloud customers, and billions of Google users worldwide.
   

  

 We're the driving force behind Google's groundbreaking innovations, empowering the development of our cutting\\-edge AI models, delivering unparalleled computing power to global services, and providing the essential platforms that enable developers to build the future. From software to hardware our teams are shaping the future of world\\-leading hyperscale computing, with key teams working on the development of our TPUs, Vertex AI for Google Cloud, Google Global Networking, Data Center operations, systems research, and much more.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Write and test product or system development code.
* Participate in, or lead design reviews with peers and stakeholders to decide amongst available technologies.
* Review code developed by other developers and provide feedback to ensure best practices (e.g., style guidelines, checking code in, accuracy, testability, and efficiency).
* Contribute to existing documentation or educational content and adapt content based on product/program updates and user feedback.
* Triage product or system issues and debug/track/resolve by analyzing the sources of issues and the impact on hardware, network, or service operations and quality.


 Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .
